topStories.description: The personnel include troops from NATO allies, along with partner countries such as Austria and Australia

topStories.title: US Education Department transfers student loan management to Treasury Dept — Here's what changes for borrowers
topStories.description: The US Education Department has transferred student loans to the Treasury Department, marking a significant step toward the Trump administration's dismantling of the agency. Here's what changes for the borrowers:

topStories.title: "Same Script, Different Stage": Iran Invokes Vietnam War In US Critique
topStories.description: Seyed Abbas Araghchi, writing on his official X account, claimed American officials had once sought to project confidence even as the Vietnam War turned against them.

topStories.title: No Safe Havens For 'Enemies': Iran Warns Of Strikes Even In Tourist Areas
topStories.description: Iran escalates rhetoric amid ongoing US-Israel strikes, warning of global retaliation even as its military leadership faces heavy losses.

topStories.title: Artefact Found In Mexico Suggests Early Arrival Of Europeans In America
topStories.description: This artifact, known as the Tecacix-Calixtlahuaca Head, is a terracotta statue of a bearded man.

topStories.title: U.S. accuses Iran’s government of operating hacktivist group that hacked Stryker
topStories.description: The U.S. Justice Department said an Iranian security ministry operates the fake activist persona known as Handala, which claimed responsibility for the destructive hack targeting medical tech giant Stryker.

topStories.title: Did Chuck Norris suffer heart attack? Fact check on theories swirling around martial arts master’s death
topStories.description: The Delta Force and Walker, Texas Ranger star was hospitalised in Hawaii after an unknown incident occurred on the island of Kauai.

topStories.title: Trump’s AI framework targets state laws, shifts child safety burden to parents
topStories.description: Trump’s AI framework pushes federal preemption of state laws, emphasizes innovation, and shifts responsibility for child safety toward parents while laying out lighter-touch rules for tech companies.

topStories.title: Trump administration sues Harvard over treatment of Jewish and Israeli students, seeks to recover billions in funds
topStories.description: The Justice Department's lawsuit against Harvard alleges the university is indifferent to antisemitism on campus. The suit aims to enforce compliance and recover taxpayer money, amidst ongoing scrutiny of university practices regarding Jewish and Israeli students.

topStories.title: Air raid sirens blare during Eid call to prayer in Kuwait
topStories.description: Air raid sirens sounded across Kuwait City as Iran targeted US assets with missiles and drones.

dailyNews.description: Trump is calling for US allies to help guarantee shipping through the Strait of Hormuz as NATO relocates Iraq personnel.

dailyNews.title: US unveils National AI Policy Framework: 7 key aspects include free speech, parents' control and personality rights
dailyNews.description: The legislative blueprint of the National Policy Framework for AI for Congress is split into seven broad categories, ranging from online safety laws for children to the protection of free speech and the streamlining of AI infrastructure.

dailyNews.title: What US-Israeli targets reveal about Iran war goals three weeks in
dailyNews.description: From degradation of military to efforts to foment unrest, targets show array of objectives but no single endgame.

dailyNews.title: Switzerland announces halt to weapons exports to US amid Iran war
dailyNews.description: Move comes as Swiss government also closed its airspace to US military flights directly linked to the Iran war.

dailyNews.title: 'Deserves perfect score': IRGC spokesman Ali Mohammad Naini lauded missile programme before US-Israel strike killed him
dailyNews.description: Prior to his death, Ali Mohammad Naini lauded Tehran's missile programme, stating it ‘deserves a perfect score’. Following Israeli airstrikes, Iran intensified its offensive, targeting oil infrastructure while vowing to continue missile production despite ongoing conflict.

dailyNews.title: US easing ban on Iranian oil would signal ‘beginning of the end of war’
dailyNews.description: The US says it is considering removing sanctions on Iranian oil stranded on tankers at sea.

dailyNews.title: Chuck Norris dies at 86: All about the Walker, Texas Ranger star’s cause of death, films and famous jokes
dailyNews.description: The 86-year-old actor was admitted to a hospital in Hawaii — just days after celebrating his 86th birthday — after an unknown incident occurred on the island of Kauai.

dailyNews.title: Cyberattack on vehicle breathalyzer company leaves drivers stranded across the US
dailyNews.description: A cyberattack on a U.S. car breathalyzer company has left drivers across the United States reportedly stranded and unable to start their vehicles.

dailyNews.title: Will Russian oil be the biggest winner in the US-Israel war on Iran?
dailyNews.description: The price of Russian Urals has soared in recent weeks. How Russia - and other energy producing nations - could profit.

dailyNews.title: ‘Not marrying a maid’: Supreme Court says wife's refusal to do household chores not cruelty
dailyNews.description: On the husband seeking divorce on grounds of cruelty, the court highlighted that times have changed and the husband must also contribute to household chores.

dailyNews.title: ‘Cowards’: Donald Trump hits out at ‘paper tiger’ NATO over lack of support for Iran war
dailyNews.description: US President Donald Trump criticized NATO allies for their insufficient backing in the war against Iran, claiming that NATO is ineffective without American support.

dailyNews.title: Zelenskyy says Ukraine wants timeline for next round of Russia talks
dailyNews.description: Volodymyr Zelenskyy says 'clear dates' needed as Ukrainian negotiators prepare for discussions in US.

topStories.description: Senate Republicans debate the SAVE America Act despite lacking votes, as Trump threatens to withhold endorsements from GOP members who oppose the bill.

topStories.description: An immigration expert says Iran's instability is unlikely to trigger a Syrian- or Afghan-style refugee surge, as the Trump admin signals no intake plan.

topStories.description: For the first time since the U.S. launched a war on Iran, this week top intelligence officials spoke publicly about the conflict.

topStories.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and President Donald Trump have said the families of service members who died in the Iran war told them to finish the job.

dailyNews.title: Bipartisan talks to end Homeland Security standoff get serious as shutdown drags on
dailyNews.description: Top Republicans and Democrats trying to end the monthlong Department of Homeland Security shutdown huddled with White House border czar Tom Homan in the Capitol on Thursday.

dailyNews.title: Ex-FBI agents assigned to Trump cases sue Kash Patel over 'unlawful' firings
dailyNews.description: Two former FBI special agents who say they played minimal roles in the investigation that led to criminal charges against President Donald Trump have filed a lawsuit against FBI Director Kash Patel and Attorney General Pam Bondi, alleging their “unlawful” firings violate the U.S.

dailyNews.title: A Kremlin loyalist suddenly turned against Putin. A day later, he was in a psychiatric hospital.
dailyNews.description: A loyal pro-Kremlin blogger who unexpectedly denounced Russian President Vladimir Putin as “a war criminal and thief” has been admitted to a psychiatric facility.

dailyNews.title: Trump administration sues Harvard over antisemitism allegations
dailyNews.description: The Justice Department filed a new lawsuit Friday against Harvard University, saying its leadership failed to address antisemitism on campus, creating grounds for the government to freeze existing grants and seek repayment for grants already paid.

dailyNews.title: Deferring to Trump, G.O.P. Lawmakers Resist a Public Accounting on Iran
dailyNews.description: Nearly three weeks into a war that polls show is unpopular, top Republicans have yet to call administration officials to testify about it, arguing that hearings would put divisions on display.

dailyNews.title: Iran War Underscores the Diverging Aims of Trump and Netanyahu
dailyNews.description: The United States views Iran through a prism of global responsibilities and strategic goals. Israel has a more regional approach. After nearly three weeks of war, their paths are diverging.

dailyNews.title: A Diary of War From an Unlikely Author: the Son of Iran’s President
dailyNews.description: In an online journal, Yousef Pezeshkian offers readers a mix of personal anecdotes and glimpses behind the scenes as Iranian leaders are picked off one after another.

dailyNews.title: U.S. Prosecutors Are Investigating Colombia President Gustavo Petro
dailyNews.description: The Justice Department is examining whether President Gustavo Petro of Colombia has ties to drug traffickers.

dailyNews.title: Trump Administration Sues Harvard Over Accusations of Antisemitism
dailyNews.description: The administration had spent months investigating the Ivy League school. The two sides had been in talks to negotiate a settlement.

dailyNews.title: Trump Friend Asked ICE to Detain the Mother of His Child
dailyNews.description: Paolo Zampolli, a former modeling agent and a longtime Trump ally, was in a custody battle over his son. An ICE official agreed to help.

dailyNews.title: A Meatpacking Strike in Colorado Is Another Stress to Trump’s Economy
dailyNews.description: The walkout at the JBS plant in Greeley, Colo., comes as beef prices have soared, cattle supplies have plunged and immigration sweeps have stressed the labor supply.

dailyNews.title: Alexander Brothers Are Said to Seek Trump Pardons for Sex Trafficking
dailyNews.description: People representing the three brothers have reached out to a lobbyist and an influential Jewish group that have supported clemency for others. There are no signs the White House is considering a pardon.
